refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the vehicle subsystem.
FR2: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation subsystem.
FR3: Ensure reliable network link with latency guarantees.
FR4: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize vehicle communication hardware and software stack for low latency.
DP2: Implement efficient teleoperation control algorithms and data compression.
DP3: Use dedicated network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation traffic.
DP4: Design telemetry and tasking interfaces with minimal buffering and processing delays.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, DP3, and DP4 to address latency across vehicle, teleoperation, network, and interface layers.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between vehicle communication hardware and teleoperation control algorithms.
Dependency on network link performance affecting all subsystems.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are interdependent but modular enough to allow separate optimization and testing. Coupling is managed via well-defined interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unpredictable network behavior.
Assumes telemetry and tasking interfaces can be optimized without compromising functionality.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance closely and consider fallback mechanisms if latency exceeds thresholds.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes teleoperation control algorithms, data compression, and interface handling for low latency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives vehicle telemetry data and sends control commands with minimal latency.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator tasking commands for execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication over the network with QoS prioritization.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation latency specifics.
Contains vehicle communication hardware/software but limited control algorithm scope.
Primary module responsible for teleoperation latency control and interface management.
Supports safety alerts but not directly responsible for latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing latency-critical teleoperation logic and interfaces in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if vehicle communication hardware/software is not tightly integrated.
Dependency on network link performance outside Teleoperation module control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate closely with Vehicle and Network Link teams to ensure latency targets are met end-to-end.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and measurable; selected DPs logically address all sub-FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to shared communication responsibilities.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and clear ownership, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency verification is feasible via telemetry and network monitoring tools within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-critical functions to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance coordination protocols between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Define fallback strategies for network latency spikes.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet requirements and constraints with clear ownership and verification paths. No immediate need to zigzag or revise.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the vehicle subsystem.
FR2: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation subsystem.
FR3: Ensure reliable network link with latency guarantees.
FR4: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low latency.

revise.why: No significant issues detected requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry and tasking interfaces can be optimized without loss of functionality.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to unpredictable network behavior.
Integration challenges between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
